methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $9.7B - $300.0B.

data.narrative:
Mistral AI is a private-stage AI & Software company founded by Arthur Mensch, Guillaume Lample, and Timothée Lacroix, currently valued at €11.7B with $14B in total capital raised. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 4 same-sector, same-stage comparables: OpenAI ($300.0B), Anthropic ($183.0B), Anysphere ($29.3B), and Neuralink ($9.7B).

The peer-median approach yields $106.2B, reflecting the central tendency across the selected comparable set. The peer range spans $9.7B to $300.0B, illustrating the wide dispersion in valuations within the frontier AI sector at the private stage. This broad range reflects material differences in capital deployment, model capability claims, and investor appetite within the cohort.

Triangulation across available methods produces a valuation band of $90.2B (low) to $122.1B (high), with a median of $106.2B. The current €11.7B valuation sits substantially below this range, though currency conversion and timing differences between valuation events warrant consideration. Key uncertainties include the sustainability of peer valuations—particularly OpenAI and Anthropic at the high end—and the extent to which Mistral's differentiation in model architecture and commercialization justifies positioning within versus below the peer median. The single-method analysis limits conviction; additional valuation triangulation (revenue multiples, DCF, or transaction comps) would strengthen the assessment.
